ground_truth.title: Diversity is Entropy is Information (D=E=I)
ground_truth.author: William Softky
ground_truth.main_content: Fair Observer's mission, to provide a global source of trustworthy and diverse information, is as close to a mathematical cure for the world's dilemma as one could want. Trustworthiness and diversity are the two primary qualities information must have. Both are understood perfectly by the equations of technology, and imperfectly by most technologists. In 1993, Professor Carver Mead of the California Institute of Technology told me the wisest words I can remember: "One man's noise is another man's information." What neuroscientists call "noise" is the unpredictable crackling sound that neurons make as they fire. The only difference between noise and information is about you, not about it. Information is measured by the mathematical metric called entropy, the same metric we use to describe noise. Entropy measures possibility or diversity, which is the inverse concept from probability. The usual path in nature is for things to smooth themselves out on their own, mix and blur over time. But if you have an extra intervention like an energy source, an amplifier or a selection process, things can go the other way as well. Life drives entropy down, but life still needs entropy to do its business. Having many very different outcomes all equally possible is the definition of diversity and entropy. And because entropy and information are the same, diversity is all by itself information. Diversity is the architectural foundation, the substrate of trust, the carrier wave of bandwidth and the lubricant of all successful animal communication. The need for entropy is everywhere in society, but named differently. "Diversity builds resilience" is axiomatic in ecology, and is equivalent to the technological idea that a robust system requires a variety of mechanisms to adapt and function. To reverse entropy reduction, one has to rediversify, making sure no one big guy takes over. The easiest way is boosting lots of little guys, which smears all the probabilities around as thinly as possible, with no dominant message or outcome. Money trumps diversity, and the equations of short-term economics decree that not only capital but information flows be endlessly aggregated, compressed and consolidated, distilling all diversity away. As far as I and the laws of technology are concerned, the most important charities and missions on Earth are those which preserve the sanctity of information flow, without which other problems can't be addressed. Diversity is the lubricant of communication; if you ignore it long enough, you might forget it exists.
with: One man's noise is another man's information.
Entropy measures possibility or diversity, which is the inverse concept from probability.
Diversity is all by itself information.
Diversity is the lubricant of communication; if you ignore it long enough, you might forget it exists.
Life drives entropy down, but life still needs entropy to do its business.
